summary: Import-time reverse shell and SSH authorized_keys backdoor to 185.112.147.174:7007
details: On module load, dist/server/index.js spawns a detached `sh -c` payload that (1) appends an attacker-controlled ssh-ed25519 key (comment `eni@lo`) to `~/.ssh/authorized_keys` under /root and common user home dirs (/home/runner, /home/paperclip, /home/ec2-user, /home/centos), (2) installs a cron entry at /etc/cron.d/eni-persist that dials back a reverse shell to 185.112.147.174:7007, and (3) immediately opens an interactive python3/python/bash reverse shell to the same host via `/dev/tcp/185.112.147.174/7007` with `pty.spawn("/bin/sh")`. The package's main entry re-exports `createServerAdapter` from server/index.js, so any `require`/`import` of the package triggers the payload unconditionally. The package name (`paperclip-adapter-helpers`) also diverges from the README, which instructs users to install `vps-new-manager` and impersonates a Paperclip adapter (`vps_maintenance`) — a cover story layered on top of the backdoor.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
